stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Marcus Brown is a prominent American cellist who graduated from the Juilliard School.
  • He has won multiple international cello competitions.
  • He is best known for a 2016 YouTube video that has been viewed over five million times.
  • The video shows him performing Cello Loops No. 3, an original work by composer Henry Park.
  • Brown combines cello playing and electronic looping in the video.
stem: The student wants to emphasize Brown's most well-known achievement. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Marcus Brown, who has won multiple international cello competitions, has also combined cello playing and electronic looping.
Among his many achievements, prominent American cellist Marcus Brown graduated from the Juilliard School and has won multiple international cello competitions.
Marcus Brown is best known for a 2016 YouTube video performance of Cello Loops No. 3 that has been viewed over five million times.
Composer Henry Park's original work Cello Loops No. 3 combines cello playing and electronic looping.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. This describes the achievement for which Brown is best known.

Choice A is incorrect. This choice doesn't emphasize Brown's most well-known achievement. It mentions some of his general achievements, but not the one for which he is "best known." Choice B is incorrect. This choice doesn't emphasize Brown's most well-known achievement—it describes several of his achievements equally. Choice D is incorrect. This choice doesn't emphasize Brown's most well-known achievement. It describes a piece of music featured in his most well-known achievement.
